kan.svnn_condition2: SATISFIED (B-spline M2 computable from control points)

mlp.verifiable_components: 0/48 (ReLU is piecewise linear but ENTANGLED with MatMul)
mlp.da_bound: N/A (DA assumes univariate decomposition — invalid for MLP)
mlp.svnn_condition1: VIOLATED (MatMul+ReLU mixed in each layer — no clean decomposition)
mlp.svnn_condition2: PARTIAL (ReLU C^0 not C^2 — second derivative doesn't exist at 0)
mlp.svnn_condition3: VIOLATED (ReLU' ∈ {0,1} gives data-dependent Lipschitz — no a priori bound)

gap_analysis.verifiability_gap: 512 vs 0 — KAN is the ONLY architecture that admits compositional formal verification
gap_analysis.error_propagation_gap: MLP IA error 14.0x worse than KAN DA, for same error source magnitude
gap_analysis.root_cause: KAN's univariate B-spline decomposition is NECESSARY for Theorem 1's structural induction. MLP's entangled MatMul+ReLU layers prevent decomposition, making DA propagation invalid and Z3 verification impossible.
